summary: EXP-226 resolves both WIN-053 c8 moderate holes in a single atomic sentence-level patch to the EXP-219 third-horn closure sentence. (A) ISS-1410: "parameterless surface fit" is self-contradictory with paragraph 4 (which names A=62,376, C=64,852, λ=8,619 as fitted coefficients); replaced with "three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry," adopting the c8 curmudgeon recommendation option (a) verbatim. Literally accurate, preserves rhetorical force. (B) ISS-1411: "the toroidal geometry does no predictive work" overshoots (a defender can point to the two-peak functional form as minimal predictive content); softened to "does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks" per the c8 curmudgeon recommendation. Concedes the weak prediction to protect the load-bearing claim (A, C, λ all fitted, none derived). §6.1 cross-reference anchor #p6-61-0 preserved verbatim. TLDRs unchanged. detail_evidence grows by ~116 chars. Both anchors verified unique.
additional_recommendations: WIN-053 c8 flagged H4 (c7 H1 carry-forward, major): internal inconsistency between paragraph 2's "~55,000 nT measured at the geographic south pole" and paragraph 3's "~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole" comparator wording. The c8 review explicitly called this "more pressing than the H3 fix we just made." Recommend the decider queue a dedicated EXP for H4 before the next holistic WIN-053 pass. The c7 Option A recommendation (rewrite paragraph 3 to use "geographic south pole" consistently) is the correct fix.

WIN-053 c8 H3 (minor): §6.1 cross-reference phrase "observed values relabeled" does not match §6.1 section heading ("Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters"); verbatim phrase appears as Section 6.4 title ("Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions"). Either re-anchor the new sentence to §6.4 or reword the parenthetical. Non-blocking, cosmetic; defer until next WIN-053 pass.
c5 carry-forwards still open: ISS-1243, ISS-1244, ISS-1245, ISS-1246 (aperture geometry, source-vs-sink polarity, Sub-H(r) δ=6,371 km unsourced). All tracked separately; none in scope for this patch.
